name: image-plan-contract
purpose: Define the reviewable large-image planning layer that runs before image generation.
scope: Large visual assets only. Tiny option icons, payment logos, progress bars, charts, UI glyphs, and generated code visuals are not image-plan slots.
hardRules: Age group image slots are mandatory when the funnel includes age_group image_grid. They must use sourcePolicy generate, count 4, theme-matched hard plain-color background, and age-differentiated prompt briefs.
Welcome or transition hero slots are mandatory when the page map includes a welcome/transition/entry hero page with a large image requirement.

Intro hero slots are generated per intro_page. The count follows the page map; do not assume a fixed number. Intro hero assets should default to 4:3 unless a page-specific design explicitly requires a different large-image ratio.
Intro hero prompt briefs must reference that intro page's copy, trust job, and section purpose.
Conditional rich question images are driven by page visualDecision, not by product-specific page names. If visualRole is none, do not create a large image slot for that page.
Conditional page visuals must carry visualRole, visualNecessity, visualFunction, and visualDecisionReason into the image plan slot.

Summary body set is mandatory for fitness, weight, body-shaping, yoga, Pilates, strength, and wellness funnels with BMI/body result summary. It must contain one consistent person identity across four body-state variants, preferably by generating one base body-state image and using image edits for the remaining variants.
Plan generation pages must not request large generated images. Use runtime animation and testimonial/content modules instead.
Paywall result comparison must reuse summary body assets where possible. It should not trigger a separate freeform body image set.
Paywall app screenshots should use sourcePolicy app_store when App Store screenshots are available.
No slot may request tiny icons, payment logos, Stripe UI, progress bars, BMI charts, weight curves, or other code-generated UI as image-generation assets.
